_comment: Baseline of ACCEPTED parity gaps, keyed by feed id. A 'gap' is a MobilityData ERROR/WARNING notice code that our validator does NOT flag on the same feed. The parity test exits non-zero only when it finds a gap NOT listed here for that feed (a regression / newly-surfaced canonical rule). To accept a new gap intentionally, add its code to the feed's array and commit. Regenerate the suggested contents by running `npm run test:validator-parity -- --write-baseline`. INFO-severity notices are never gating. Regenerated against MobilityData v8.0.1 on 2026-07-12, when the two GTFS-Flex feeds were added; every remaining gap below is a NON-flex feed-hygiene rule (feed_info recommended fields, route_long_name containing route_short_name, stops with no stop_time). The flex feeds have ZERO flex gaps: the canonical validator and our validator agree on all 5 injected flex errors.
